description_long: This is a rectangular, freestanding shelving unit constructed from dark wood with a rich, natural grain pattern. The structure features a grid of open square compartments arranged in four rows and three columns, providing twelve equally sized storage spaces. The sides and shelves are made of thick wooden panels, giving the unit a sturdy and robust appearance. The back of the unit is open, allowing visibility and access from both the front and rear. The finish is smooth and slightly glossy, highlighting the wood's texture and color. This type of shelving is commonly used for organizing books, decorative items, or storage bins in urban environments such as offices, lobbies, or residential spaces.
description: A dark wood, freestanding shelving unit with twelve open square compartments and a smooth, slightly glossy finish. The grid design and robust panels make it ideal for organizing items in urban interiors.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full grid of twelve open square compartments and the wood grain pattern.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thickness of the wooden panels and the depth of the shelving unit.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually similar to the front, with open compartments and no backing panel.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the panel thickness and depth.
